title: Rare-Disease Screen: Resampling/Reweighting Policy for a Fixed Classifier
strategies: uniform weights: do nothing about the imbalance; the fixed classifier collapses onto the healthy majority and the rare subtypes get near-zero recall (reproduces the evaluator's weak baseline ~0.1)
square-root inverse-frequency reweighting: weight each patient by 1/sqrt(class count); a gentle rebalance that beats uniform but under-corrects, leaving macro-F1 on the table

full inverse-frequency (class-balanced) reweighting: weight by N/(K*class_count) so every class contributes equal training mass; the textbook macro-F1 fix, recovers rare-class recall and lands well above the gentle rule

difficulty-aware / synthetic-minority policies: estimate per-class separability from the biomarker features and up-weight the harder rare subtype, or synthesize/jitter minority points, to push toward (but not reach) the balanced-ideal ceiling
